chunk_summary: This section outlines minimum-contact and hybrid delivery models for obesity management in Indian outpatient settings, emphasizing structured, frequent follow-ups, group sessions, and telehealth to meet IHBLT dose principles despite resource constraints.
learning_objectives: Understand the structure of minimum-contact and hybrid delivery models for obesity management in Indian OPD settings.
Learn how to implement IHBLT dose principles through structured follow-ups and group sessions.
Identify strategies to enhance patient engagement and adherence using telehealth and problem-solving counseling.
key_facts: Initial baseline visit should last 20–30 minutes to confirm anthropometry, plan comorbidity screening, and set initial goals.
Follow-up visits should be 10–12 minutes long, scheduled every 2–4 weeks, with 4–6 visits recommended.
Each follow-up visit should include weight/BMI tracking, process indicator review, a focused module, and barrier-solving counseling.
Monthly group sessions (e.g., diet demos, activity starters) and WhatsApp reinforcement can increase treatment dose.
Telehealth check-ins are particularly useful during exam months or for interim troubleshooting.
reference_quote: ‘A minimum viable model can still preserve dose logic if it is structured and frequent.’ (AAP Clinical Practice Guideline, 2023)
document_key_topics: Minimum-contact delivery models for obesity management
Telehealth and digital reinforcement in Indian settings
